children.value: At this point you could avoid everything else by writing your own hook and run the code above to see what operations are being made, and what value is being returned, after which you could solve for the flag.

children.value: You may or may not recognize this as a
Now we need to actually decode what
We can use hooks, or simply make all the operators return strings.
Two of the functions may appear complicated but if you test them, they are just addition and subtraction.

props.className: language-js shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai
props.code:
const operators = {
    CzhQSPrjvxQ7vfEm: 'function (x, y) {\r\n' +
        '        let a = y[x.pop()], b = y[x.pop()]\r\n' +
        '        return `${b}[${a}]`\r\n' +
        '    }',
    spCiy27WpEuz0bAh: 'function (x, y) {\r\n        return `${y[x.pop()]}.charCodeAt(0)`\r\n    }',
    mTvc3QBx6ieTIzEA: 'function (x, y) {\r\n        return `!${y[x.pop()]}`\r\n    }',
    I4TO8mHsfL6Tic7v: 'function (x, y) {\r\n' +
        '        let a = y[x.pop()], b = y[x.pop()]\r\n' +
        '        return `${b} % ${a}`\r\n' +
        '    }',
        ...
};

children.value: Now run it but with the arguments wrapped.
